Metadata.title: SaaS Starter | Next.js, Supabase, Stripe
Metadata.description: Generic, production-ready SaaS starter with Next.js, Supabase auth, and Stripe subscriptions.

errors.unableSocialLogin: Unable to continue with social login.

messages.accountCreated: Account created. Check your inbox to verify email if confirmation is enabled.

description.login: Sign in to manage your SaaS subscription.
description.signup: Start with secure auth and billing-ready infrastructure.

hero.title: Launch a production-ready SaaS in days, not months.
hero.description: Skip months of platform work. Auth, team access, seat-based billing, support email, and AI-ready APIs are already wired so you can focus on the product customers pay for.
hero.shipNowDescription: App and API flows mapped to a real SaaS launch.
hero.shipItem1: Signup, login, forgot-password, reset-password, and protected dashboard routes
hero.shipItem2: Team onboarding with invites, acceptance, role management, and ownership transfer
hero.shipItem3: Seat-based Stripe checkout, billing portal, plan changes, and webhook syncing
hero.shipItem4: Support request API with Resend delivery and authenticated sender context
hero.shipItem5: Optional AI chat route with streaming responses, budgeting, and plan-aware access
hero.shipItem6: Security defaults including CSRF checks, CSP headers, request IDs, and rate limiting

gettingStarted.title: How to get up-and-running
gettingStarted.description: Follow the setup in the README to go from fresh clone to a working SaaS app with real auth and billing flows.
gettingStarted.step1Text: Install dependencies, copy `.env.example` to `.env.local`, and set your required values.
gettingStarted.step2Text: Apply `supabase/schema.sql`, add Stripe price IDs + webhook secret, and configure Resend.
gettingStarted.step3Text: Update branding, pricing, and product logic, then deploy to Vercel with optional cron jobs.

whyStarter.label: Why this starter
whyStarter.title: Built for real SaaS operations, not just demos.
whyStarter.description: The codebase includes production-minded patterns for teams, subscriptions, support, AI access controls, and operational cron endpoints.

bestPractices.title: Built-in best practices
bestPractices.description: Core paths are implemented with safety and maintainability in mind, so you can extend confidently.
bestPractices.securityDefaultsText: CSRF protection, CSP, strict API headers, and request-aware middleware are included.
bestPractices.teamBillingText: Stripe subscriptions are team-scoped with seat reconciliation, portal access, and plan changes.
bestPractices.operationalText: Cron routes reconcile seats and clean webhook data, with token-based authorization.

stack.title: Inside the stack
stack.description: Next.js App Router with clear boundaries across UI routes, API handlers, and shared platform libraries.
stack.authText: Supabase SSR auth helpers, invite acceptance flow, team context, and account settings are already connected.
stack.billingText: Checkout, portal, and change-plan APIs are backed by webhook dedupe and subscription sync logic.
stack.opsText: AI chat, Resend support email, and optional Intercom + Sentry integrations are ready to configure.

pricing.title: Seat-based pricing from Stripe
pricing.description: Configure Starter, Growth, and Pro price IDs once, and the landing page and billing flows stay in sync.

ctaFaq.title: Ready to ship your SaaS faster?
ctaFaq.description: Start with proven auth, team, billing, and API infrastructure, then build the product experience that makes your business unique.
ctaFaq.q1: What is included out of the box?
ctaFaq.a1: A production-oriented SaaS foundation with Supabase auth, team invites and roles, seat-based Stripe billing, dashboard routes, support email via Resend, and security middleware already wired in.
ctaFaq.q2: Do I need to build auth and billing myself?
ctaFaq.a2: No. Login, signup, password reset, protected routes, checkout, plan changes, billing portal, and webhook handling are already implemented so you can focus on product features.
ctaFaq.q3: How are teams and permissions handled?
ctaFaq.a3: Teams support invite flows and role-based access (`owner`, `admin`, `member`), with ownership transfer and guarded membership operations handled through API routes and database policies.
ctaFaq.a4: Yes. `/api/ai/chat` is included with streaming responses, CSRF checks, rate limiting, and configurable access rules by paid status or plan when `OPENAI_API_KEY` is set.
